spans.name: assistant-message
spans.output: So the map delete IS there. Let me re-read the issue more carefully and check if the problem might be in the dispose() method itself:

spans.name: tool:bash
assistant-message
spans.output:
Aha! In the `dispose()` method, the code calls `reset(this._domNode)` which removes all child nodes, but it does NOT dispose the views in the `this.views` Map, nor does it clear the Map itself. This is the memory leak! When the `EditorGutter` is disposed (when closing editors), the `views` Map still holds references to all the `ManagedGutterItemView` objects, which in turn hold references to the `gutterItemView` (which are disposables), preventing them from being garbage collected.

Let me look
